title: Create and configure HttpClient
instruction:
Create an HttpClient and set headers.

Run this.
successMessage: HttpClient is reusable — create once, reuse across calls. using disposes it properly.

title: GET a string
instruction:
`GetStringAsync` fetches a URL and returns the body as a string.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Use GetStringAsync to fetch the URL.
Use await before the async call.

successMessage: GetStringAsync is the simplest way to fetch text content. It throws HttpRequestException on network errors or non-2xx responses.

title: Check status code
instruction:
`GetAsync` gives you the full response including status code.

Run this.
successMessage: GetAsync returns HttpResponseMessage. StatusCode is the HTTP status. IsSuccessStatusCode is true for 200-299.

title: Deserialise JSON
instruction:
Use `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>` to parse JSON into a C# object.

Run this.
successMessage: JsonSerializer.Deserialize maps JSON to a C# type. PropertyNameCaseInsensitive handles mixed-case JSON keys.

title: Serialise to JSON
instruction:
`JsonSerializer.Serialize<T>` converts an object to JSON.

Run this.
successMessage: WriteIndented=true produces formatted JSON. Use it for debug output. Leave it false for API responses to save bandwidth.

title: POST with JSON body
instruction:
Send a POST request with a JSON body.

Run this.
successMessage: PostAsJsonAsync serialises the object and sets Content-Type: application/json automatically. It is the clean way to POST JSON.

title: Error handling
instruction:
Handle HTTP errors gracefully.

Run this.
successMessage: EnsureSuccessStatusCode throws HttpRequestException for non-2xx responses. Catch it to handle 404, 500, etc. gracefully.

title: Query parameters and headers
instruction:
Build URLs with query strings and add headers.

Run this.
successMessage: HttpUtility.ParseQueryString builds a properly encoded query string. DefaultRequestHeaders sets headers on all requests from this client.
